title: structure.json — song sections and cut/beat alignment
description: Sections fuse audio-novelty segmentation with lyric-repetition clustering; each carries its evidence and confidence. Beat/bar indices refer to the beat grid in audio_features.json (this document owns no clock of its own). The editing block exists only for video inputs.

sections.description: Sorted, non-overlapping, covering the full track duration. Boundaries snapped to the nearest downbeat.

label.description: Functional labels when lyric evidence supports them; generic section_a..d (by similarity cluster) when it does not (e.g. instrumental tracks).

occurrence.description: 1-based count among sections sharing this label (chorus #2 => label 'chorus', occurrence 2). Present when a label repeats.

start_bar_index.description: Derived bar number (see audio_features.beats). Approximate when downbeat_method is 'assumed_4_4_phase_fit'.

evidence.description: Which detectors support this section. Both = strong; audio_novelty alone is common for instrumentals and intros.

editing.description: Cut/beat alignment metrics. Present only for video inputs.

on_beat_window_seconds.description: Half-window used for the on-beat classification below (config, default 0.07). Consumers wanting a different definition should use the per-cut offsets instead.

per_section.description: Parallel to sections (same order, same length).

cuts.description: Every shot boundary (excluding t=0), sorted, so the mapping engine can apply its own on-beat definition.

offset_seconds.description: cut time minus nearest beat time; negative = cut lands early.
